headline: Goldman Sachs Warns Qatar LNG Damage Could 'Makes The Process Very Painful,' Pushing Global Gas Prices Up 50–100%
summary: Goldman Sachs warned that damage to Qatar's liquefied natural gas (LNG) infrastructure could push global gas prices 50–100% higher, sparking a potential energy crisis ahead of winter. Qatar LNG Attack Disrupts Global Supply The concern centers on QatarEnergy's Ras Laffan Industrial City, the world's largest LNG production site, which was knocked offline by attacks. Qatar supplies roughly one-fifth of the global LNG market. "There is a risk that this just drags so much that it makes the process v

headline: How The Colgate Palmolive (CL) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Targets And 2030 Plan
summary: Colgate-Palmolive’s modeled fair value has been trimmed slightly from US$97.26 to US$96.68 per share, a change of about US$0.58 that keeps the overall valuation view broadly intact while acknowledging updated inputs. That small adjustment sits against mixed Street commentary, where some analysts highlight the long term 2030 plan and others flag cost and valuation risks that have led to price targets moving both higher and lower. Read on to see how these shifting targets, recent news, and new...

headline: AI Juggernaut Rumbles on Even as Markets Whipsaw
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The artificial intelligence credit juggernaut keeps pushing forward as the relentless demand for exposure to the industry trumps fears that the conflict in the Middle East is causing energy prices and inflation to rise. Despite the geopolitical headwinds, Wall Street was still able to successfully stitch together tens of billions of dollars in funding to underpin the AI boom in recent weeks. The tumult may even be making AI-linked high-grade debt more appealing this year as invest

headline: IBM Pays $17 Million To Settle DEI Allegations In DOJ's First False Claims Act Win
summary: IBM agrees to pay $17 million to settle federal allegations related to DEI hiring practices, marking the first resolution under the DOJ's Civil Rights Fraud Initiative.
